channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Northern Territory child protection reforms criticised by Indigenous groups
item.description: The Northern Territory Government has announced significant changes to child protection laws, focusing on child safety as the primary consideration when assessing whether children should be removed fr...

item.title: President Mahama Returns to Ghana After Participating in Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi
item.description: President John Dramani Mahama has returned to Ghana after taking part in the Africa Forward Summit held in Nairobi, Kenya. The summit was co-hosted by Kenyan President William Ruto and French Presiden...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Vision 2050 with TZS 12.50 Trillion Budget for First Five-Year Plan
item.description: Tanzania, under the leadership of Prime Minister Dr. Mwigulu Nchemba, has officially started implementing Vision 2050, aiming for a USD 1 trillion economy and a per capita income of USD 7,000 by the y...

item.title: MSF Reports Rising Malnutrition and Disease Challenges Across Nigeria
item.description: Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) don sound serious alarm say malnutrition, recurring disease outbreaks, and poor maternal healthcare dey worsen for Nigeria. For their 2025 Country Activity Report, dem t...

item.title: Atishi Explains Kejriwal's Comments on Gen Z Were Misinterpreted
item.description: Delhi's political scene witnessed some confusion recently after a remark made by Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal about Generation Z was interpreted by some as inciting violence. Atishi, a senior leader...

item.title: Pope Appoints Immigration-Critical Pastor to Lead Naples Diocese in Florida
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has appointed Father Emilio Biosca Agüero, known for his criticism of the Trump administration's deportation policies, as the new Bishop of Naples, Florida. Biosca Agüero has led the Shri...

item.title: Joyce DeWitt’s Role as Janet Wood in “Three’s Company” Highlighted Through Vintage Portraits
item.description: Joyce DeWitt played the role of Janet Wood on ABC’s popular sitcom “Three’s Company” from 1977 to 1984. Her character, known for her iconic brunette bob, was the pragmatic and reliable roommate who ba...

item.title: Northwest Redistricting Challenges and Potential Maps Ahead of 2026 Elections
item.description: While much of the national attention on gerrymandering has focused on battleground and heavily redistricted states, the Northwest faces unique constraints. Smaller states like Alaska and Wyoming have ...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Wardrobe Polaroids of Catherine O’Hara on SCTV in 1983
item.description: In 1983, Catherine O’Hara, the celebrated Canadian actress, was a key performer on the iconic sketch comedy show Second City Television (SCTV). A newly surfaced collection of wardrobe Polaroids provid...

item.title: Kwara Prophet Gets Life Sentence for Raping Three Underage Church Members
item.description: The Kwara State High Court for Ilorin don sentence one Prophet Ajiboye Abayomi Olayinka, the General Overseer of Mission House of Divine Land of Joy and Prayer Ministry, to three life imprisonments fo...

item.title: Scottish Citizen Sentenced in Ukraine for Admitting to Russian Espionage
item.description: A Scottish man has been sentenced to prison in Ukraine after confessing to conducting espionage activities on behalf of Russia. Ukrainian authorities stated that the individual had gathered sensitive ...

item.title: BJP Achieves Major Wins in Haryana Municipal Elections, Including Sampla
item.description: In the Haryana municipal corporation elections held recently, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) achieved a sweeping victory across several key cities. The party won major posts in Panchkula, Ambala, an...

item.title: Federal Budget 2026 Introduces Housing Tax Reforms with Limited Impact on Affordability
item.description: The 2026-27 federal budget introduces significant changes to housing tax policy, aiming to improve home ownership rates. Key measures include limiting negative gearing to newly built properties and re...

item.title: Boy rescued after being trapped under train at North Melbourne station, causing rail delays
item.description: Authorities in North Melbourne responded to a serious incident involving a young boy who became trapped under a train at the local station. Emergency services acted quickly to rescue the child, prompt...

item.title: Rivers State Government Reiterates Support for Quality Education and Human Capital Development at IAUE Convocation
item.description: The Rivers State Government under Governor Sir Siminalayi Fubara has once again shown strong commitment to quality education and human capital development in the state. During the 44th Convocation Cer...

item.title: Understanding the Factors Behind Africa’s High Out-of-School Youth Numbers
item.description: Despite widespread adoption of universal basic education policies across Africa, over 100 million children and adolescents remain out of school, a figure that has increased from 90 million in 2014. Wh...

item.title: STAMICO and Singapore Partner to Develop Graphite Mining in Mirerani
item.description: Tanzania's State Mining Corporation (STAMICO) has entered a joint venture with Singapore-based Planet One Natural Resources Holdings to develop three graphite mining licenses in Mirerani, Manyara Regi...

item.title: Ghana Arranges Safe Return for Citizens Amid South Africa Xenophobic Violence
item.description: Ghana don plan to bring back around 300 of its citizens wey dey South Africa after another round of xenophobic attacks against migrants from sub-Saharan Africa. The Ghana High Commission dey organise ...

item.title: Republicans Struggle to Address Affordability Amid Internal Controversies
item.description: As the 2026 midterm elections approach, Republicans face growing criticism for failing to focus on affordability, the top concern for American voters. According to Politico, the party is being consume...

item.title: Bank of Canada sees AI as transformative but not yet a threat to jobs
item.description: The Bank of Canada has stated that, so far, artificial intelligence (AI) has not led to widespread job losses in Canada, although its continued adoption could bring permanent changes to the economy. M...
